pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB (Vital Signs DataBase) - open dataset containing 6,388 intraoperative cases from Seoul National University Hospital (SNUH), Seoul, Republic of Korea
pi_items.source: Web search and verification: VitalDB is a real, publicly accessible dataset from SNUH (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 licensed) containing high-resolution intraoperative vital signs data from noncardiac surgeries. Confirmed via vitaldb.net and Nature Scientific Data publication (PMC9178032)

pi_items.source: vitaldb.VitalFile shows all three anesthetic monitoring tracks present among 81 total tracks

privileged_info_summary: Case 0003 is from the VitalDB open dataset (Seoul National University Hospital), containing 6,388 intraoperative cases. This specific case is a noncardiac surgery lasting 1 hour 13 minutes 14 seconds. The vital file (0003.vital) contains 81 tracks including all expected anesthetic monitoring channels: ECG_II and ECG_V5 (electrocardiography), PLETH (pulse oximetry), COMPLIANCE (lung compliance), INSP_SEVO and EXP_SEVO (sevoflurane concentrations), plus airway pressures (PAMB_MBAR, MAWP_MBAR, PPLAT_MBAR) and other vital monitoring parameters. The data is real, anonymized perioperative vital signs collected from Seoul National University Hospital, South Korea (August 2016 - June 2017).
